jobs.title: Sales Internship at genesisONE
jobs.description: Employer: genesisONE Expires: 07/10/2026 Overview:genesisONE is a bold nationwide managed print services leader that meets and exceeds our customers’ rapidly evolving needs. As the result of our evolution from Genesis Technologies, we emerged with a new name: genesisONE. Together we are ONE and a one-of-a-kind managed print services expert meeting the needs of a new hybrid workforce, positioning ourselves and our customers for growth and success. genesisONE is seeking a talented and dynamic individual to serve as a New Business Development Account Executive in our Chicago Loop office. For more information on our company, please visit www.mygenesis1.com.Read further if you want to:• Be creative! Develop lead generation strategies to create your prospect pipeline• Do a little detective work. Research local companies and search for those that fit our “ideal customer criteria”• Be social. Spend your days talking to people. Telephone prospects and cold call on business owners and C-level executives.• Market the latest technology. Use or complete office and IT technology product line to grow a portfolio of profitable business• Make a difference! Small and medium-sized businesses are looking for ways to do things faster, easier, more sustainable and moreproductive. Help keep businesses grow. Your insight and assessment can help them.• Be part of a successful sales team. A successful sales team and committed leadership will support your efforts.You will work hard, you will have fun and you’ll enjoy:• A compensation plan that rewards success• Continuous training to advance your professional development and grow your sales career• A great work environment with a team of people who enjoy working together!To be successful in this role you will need:• Be a people person. Talking to people is what you’ll do so you’ll need to be a natural at it and enjoy it• Have a professionally persistent ability to make repeated calls, emails and social media connections to gain access to C-level buyers.• The ability to look for new customers and new business within existing customer accounts• An affinity toward technology and a belief in what it can do to improve our daily lives, then take that and translate that into a value ourcustomers can see using to improve their business processes• The ability to mine the CRM system (i.e. Salesforce) for new opportunities and to manage the sales funnel• Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills• Skillful problem-solving capabilities• Software and reporting tools experience• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and WordOur investment doesn’t stop with the customer; it carries over to our employees. We seek a long-term relationship to grow your career. That’s why genesisONE has won the “Chicago’s Best and Brightest Companies to Work For®” award for the 13th time in 2024 and the “Nation’s Best and Brightest Companies to Work For®” award for the 5th time

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Summer Social Media Intern at KHOI LLC
jobs.description: Employer: KHOI LLC Expires: 07/10/2026 KHOI is looking for a Summer Social Media Intern to join our team from July 1 through August 30. We're looking for someone who lives and breathes social media, loves creating content, and is excited to help build and grow the KHOI community both online and in person. What You'll Do Assist with content creation and photoshoots Capture behind-the-scenes moments and brand content Create and post social media content across platforms Travel with the team to pop-up events and activations Engage with and help grow our online community Stay on top of social media trends, conversations, and emerging platform features Help ideate creative ways to translate KHOI campaigns across Instagram, TikTok, and other digital channels Support social media planning, content calendars, and campaign execution Represent and embody the KHOI brand What We're Looking For Passion for social media, fashion, and storytelling Strong understanding of Instagram, TikTok, and emerging platforms A creative thinker with a strong sense of what resonates online Comfortable being on-site at shoots and events Organized, proactive, and collaborative Available July 1 – August 30 Willing to travel to local pop-up events as needed Must be based in the Chicago area and available to attend in-person shoots, events, and team activities If you're excited to create content, spot trends before they happen, and help shape the voice of a growing brand, we'd love to hear from you. How to Apply Please submit the following: Resume Cover letter Links to your social media profiles and/or portfolio (if applicable) Your cover letter is mandatory and should tell us about who you are, your relevant experience, what excites you about social media and content creation, and why you're interested in KHOI. We want to get to know you beyond your resume and understand what perspective, creativity, and energy you would bring to the team. Please email all application materials to HR@shopkhoi.com with the subject line "Summer Social Media Internship Application – [Your Name]".

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Creative Residency — Marketing & Content | Parasol Projects at Parasol Projects
jobs.description: Employer: Parasol Projects Expires: 07/11/2026 Creative Residency — Marketing & Content | Parasol ProjectsAbout Parasol Projects Parasol Projects is New York City's full-service pop-up and experiential activation agency. We've produced over 1,400 activations across 10 locations in SoHo, Nolita, NoHo, Williamsburg, and Miami, partnering with fashion, beauty, lifestyle, CPG, and tech brands — from emerging DTC labels to globally recognized names like New Balance, TikTok, Ipsy, FabFitFun, and KraneShares — to bring their brands to life in physical space.About the Residency This isn't a coffee-and-spreadsheets internship. The Parasol Creative Residency is a hands-on, semester-long placement inside a fast-moving experiential agency where real brands are activating every week. You'll work directly with our Marketing Director, contribute to live campaigns, and leave with a professional portfolio and real industry skills.What you'll doPhotograph and film brand activations across our NYC locationsEdit photo and video content for Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, and client deliverablesAssist with social media scheduling and community managementSupport pop-up listing updates, Google Business Profile uploads, and content filingContribute to email campaign execution and CRM managementAssist with press tasks including press releases and media outreachCollaborate on campaign strategy and marketing reportingWhat you'll gainHands-on experience working with established and emerging brands across fashion, beauty, lifestyle, and techA professional content portfolio built across 20+ live brand activationsReal press and PR experiencePractical skills in marketing metrics, analytics, and campaign reportingDirect mentorship from an AI-enabled Marketing Director with agency and luxury hospitality experienceA named credential — Parasol Creative Resident — for your CV and LinkedInDetailsDuration: One semester (approx. 15–18 weeks), with potential to renewHours: 15–20 hours per week, flexible around your class scheduleLocation: On-site at activations across NYC + hybrid remote for editing and adminStipend: Monthly stipend providedStart date: September 2026You're a good fit if you areStudying marketing, communications, fashion, journalism, media, or a related fieldA strong visual eye — confident with a phone or cameraFamiliar with Instagram, TikTok, and basic editing tools (CapCut, Lightroom, or similar)Organized, proactive, and comfortable in a fast-moving environmentGenuinely excited about experiential marketing, brand activations, and the NYC creative scene

jobs.title: Marketing Internship - Paid at TOA-SE
jobs.description: Employer: TOA-SE Expires: 07/11/2026 Job Title: Marketing Internship - PaidCompany: TOA SE, Inc.Location: Nicholasville, KYEmployment Type: Part-Time Internship, Co-op (Flexible Days/Hours – No more than 3 days a week, 8:30am-3:30pm with 30-60 minutes of unpaid lunch)About Us: TOA SE, Inc. is a leader in automation and robotics specializing in the design and development of advanced automation systems. Our commitment to quality, safety, and innovation makes us a trusted partner for manufacturers seeking cutting-edge automation solutions. Visit our website at www.toa-se.com to learn more about our work and the industries we serve.Position Overview: We are seeking a motivated and creative Marketing Intern to join our team. This position is ideal for a university student pursuing a degree in marketing, communications, or a related field, who wants to gain hands-on experience in a dynamic technology-driven company. The internship will provide an opportunity to develop and showcase your marketing skills while contributing to impactful projects.Responsibilities:Social Media Management: Create social media posts to showcase TOA SE’s capabilities and project successes, increasing online presence and engagement.Website Updates: Update and maintain content on TOA SE’s company website to ensure it reflects our latest projects and services.Brand Growth: Collaborate with our sales team on strategies to expand TOA SE’s brand visibility in the automation and robotics industry.Support Tasks: Assist with diverse graphic design tasks for both print and website platforms as needed.Work Schedule:Flexible hours within the 8:30 AM – 3:30 PM window, Monday through Friday, up to 3 days a week.No weekend work required.Internship duration: Flexible, with potential for extensionQualifications:Currently enrolled in a university program with a focus on Marketing, Communications, or related fields.Strong understanding of social media platforms and digital marketing trends.Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite required. Video and photo editing experience preferred.Excellent written and verbal communication skills.Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.Interest in working in an innovative, technology-focused industry.Why Join?Gain hands-on experience in marketing within the growing technology field of automation and robotics.Opportunity to build a portfolio showcasing your creative and strategic marketing skills.Be part of an innovative company at the forefront of automation and advanced manufacturing.

jobs.description: Employer: George Wealth Management Affiliated with the Cetera Advisors Network LLC Expires: 07/11/2026 LinkedIn Business Development & Marketing InternAbout George Wealth ManagementGeorge Wealth Management helps successful professionals, executives, and business owners answer important financial questions:Am I actually making progress?Is my business helping me build the future I want?If work became optional, what am I moving toward?We use a scorecard-based process to help people gain clarity and identify opportunities across their financial lives.Position SummaryWe are seeking a detail-oriented and professional LinkedIn Business Development & Marketing Intern to help build relationships with executives, business owners, CPAs, and attorneys throughout the Southeast.This role focuses on LinkedIn prospecting, lead organization, CRM management, and marketing support. jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: INTERNSHIP: Sales Development Intern at Smartmatic USA
jobs.description: Employer: Smartmatic USA Expires: 07/12/2026 About SmartmaticSmartmatic is a global technology company delivering end-to-end election technology and services to governments and election authorities worldwide. With a presence in more than 25 countries, we are committed to building trust in democracy through innovation, integrity, and excellence.Position OverviewSmartmatic is seeking a driven Sales Development Intern to fuel the growth of our U.S. sales team. This is a front-line, hands-on internship focused on generating and qualifying new business opportunities for our election technology products. You'll learn the top of the sales funnel from the ground up — researching target jurisdictions, running multi-channel outreach, and booking qualified meetings for the U.S. Sales Director — while working alongside and learning directly from seasoned sales professionals with deep experience in the election technology industry. The ideal candidate is competitive, personable, organized, and eager to develop real sales skills by engaging government decision-makers by phone, email, and LinkedIn.Key ResponsibilitiesGenerate qualified sales pipeline through outbound prospecting — cold and warm calls, email sequences, and LinkedIn outreach — to county and state election offices.Research target jurisdictions to identify key decision-makers (County Clerks, Election Administrators, Registrars, Supervisors of Elections, Boards of Elections) and map procurement timelines and budget cycles.Qualify inbound and outbound leads against defined criteria and book discovery calls and product demos for the Sales Director.Follow up on conference and event leads to convert interest into scheduled meetings.Maintain accurate, up-to-date records of all activity, contacts, and opportunities in the CRM.Track outreach metrics against weekly and monthly activity targets (calls, emails, meetings booked, pipeline sourced).Support the sales team with competitive research and account intelligence as needed.What You'll GainDirect mentorship from seasoned sales professionals with extensive experience in government and election technology sales.Hands-on training in modern B2G sales development, prospecting, and pipeline generation.Real exposure to the full sales cycle and the U.S. election technology market.QualificationsCurrently enrolled in a degree program (Business, Political Science, Public Administration, Communications, or related field).Strong verbal and written communication skills, with a professional, confident phone manner and comfort making cold and warm calls to government offices.Highly organized, self-motivated, and goal-oriented, with a competitive drive to hit activity targets.Prior sales, customer-facing, or business development experience a plus.Eagerness to learn and take coaching from experienced sales mentors.Hands-on experience with AI productivity tools such as ChatGPT and Claude.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Performance Media Intern at Collective Measures
jobs.description: Employer: Collective Measures Expires: 07/12/2026 We are seeking a Performance Media Intern for a paid internship who is proactive, detail oriented, and eager to learn the ins-and-outs of digital marketing. This position will support the Performance Media Team, aid in client tactics, and learn the fundamentals of PPC advertising. This internship is available immediately and offers an opportunity to convert into a full-time position with the agency. _RESPONSIBILITIES: Research for and assist in creating and optimizing paid search and paid social campaignsWork alone or with teams to develop, implement, report on, and analyze PPC campaigns top to bottom across multiple websites and channelsSummarize and communicate the status of all campaigns with appropriate internal contacts.Work with project manager to create and ensure timely delivery of project schedulesReport developing search trends, tactics, and opportunities to internal team and clients, with occasional assistance in POV (Point of View) documentsWork independently as well as with a team in a fast paced and goal-oriented environment _REQUIREMENTS: Willingness to learn and use Google Ads, Bing Ads, Google Analytics, SearchForce, Skai, and Google Webmaster Tools. Any current certifications are a plusSelf-starter approach to work, with a positive attitude, to consistently meet and exceed objectives and take on more responsibilityStrong written and verbal communication skillsA data-driven, analytical approach to problem solvingPreferred experience in digital media, especially in managing paid search campaignsStrong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlinesStrong experience with Microsoft ExcelMust be able to read, write and speak English proficientlyMust be able to use a computer keyboard and telephone (with or without accommodation) _PAY TRANSPARENCY + INTERNSHIP BENEFITS:The pay rate for internships at Collective Measures is $20 per hour. Benefits available to interns include:Participation in the Employee Stock Ownership PlanCommuter stipend (if working as a hybrid employee)Participation in 401(K) Plan _ ABOUT COLLECTIVE MEASURESCollective Measures is the agency brands call when they’re ready to move past the noise. We don’t wait for certainty; we create it. Built at the intersection of media and analytics, we turn raw signals into directions that are clear, actionable, and built to scale. Our work fuses the logic of data with the momentum of conviction, shaping systems that turn constant change into measurable progress. We’re not theorists or tacticians; we’re builders. Every plan we design connects insight to execution, vision to proof. The result is marketing that doesn’t just perform, it propels. As an employee-owned agency, we think like stakeholders because we are. Collective Measures does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, age, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factors.Studies have shown that women and people of color may be less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every one of the qualifications listed. We are most interested in finding the best candidate for the job. We would encourage you to apply for a job at Collective Measures even if you don’t meet every one of our qualifications listed.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Community Intern, Columbus at Yelp
jobs.description: Employer: Yelp Expires: 07/12/2026 SummaryYelp’s Community team works in markets across the United States and Canada to bring attention to the best local businesses in their area. Through events, local partnerships, and social media, our team builds relationships with small business owners and uses their behind-the-scenes experience to show other customers what makes small businesses so special. Join the fun and begin building your five star career as a Community Intern with Yelp! You will engage in a 6-month development program, equipped with the support of both a cohort of peers spanning the US and Canada and the 1:1 mentorship from your local Community Manager. You will play an integral part in supporting activities designed to rally the Yelp community both online and off. You will gain tactical experience in social media management, event planning, and online community building, while building excitement around what's happening in your own community. What you'll do:You will work with your Community Manager to understand your market and prioritize effective messagingYou will help to plan and execute events including remarkable parties, local meet-ups, and happy hoursYou will gain practical experience in digital and social media marketing through creating and organizing social media content that aligns with regional goals around growing channels and bolstering engagement You will support the maintenance of Yelp’s local online business listings via our app and website by flagging photos, identifying media alerts and scams, and ensuring accurate business informationYou will support online community building through engaging with Yelpers via the app/website in a variety of ways What it takes to succeed:You are a current undergraduate student, college graduate, or interested in making a career change to a community building role.You currently reside in Columbus, Ohio and have reliable transportation (Required)You are at least 21 years of age (Required) You consider yourself a local expert- you know what is trending in the area and have a love for small businessesYou have experience and interest in planning and coordinating eventsYou have strong written and verbal communication skillsYou are well organized and pay attention to detailYou have experience with social media copywriting and asset coordination You are a creative problem solver who understands Yelp’s applications You have the bandwidth and flexibility to work 10-19 hours per week, Monday through Friday with the potential for some weekend workYou have the ability to lift 10 pounds without assistance What you'll get:Compensation range for this position is $13.00 - $16.00 per hour.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Sales Internship - Beauty at Volaant
jobs.description: Employer: Volaant Expires: 07/12/2026 Sales & Marketing Internship Volaant — Clinical Luxury SkincareLocation: RemoteDuration: Full-time summer internship, June 12 – September 12, 2026Compensation: CommissionReports to: Founder & CEOPath forward: Strong performers will be offered a paid part-time or full-time role after the internship concludes About VolaantVolaant is a clinical luxury skincare brand built on pharmaceutical-grade formulation and refined aesthetic. Our products serve the most discerning consumers and the most exacting professional channels — board-certified dermatologists, plastic surgeons, and medical aesthetic spas across the country.We launch summer 2026 with a founder-led sales strategy that puts our products directly into the hands of leading clinical practices. This internship gives you a seat at the table for that launch. About the OpportunityThis is a real sales role with real responsibility. You will be trained directly by our Founder & CEO — a seasoned, exited serial entrepreneur with two decades of experience scaling companies from launch through exit, including hands-on sales leadership at the CRO and CEO level.You will learn how to sell into one of the most sophisticated buyer audiences in the country: highly educated physician-owners and medical professionals who demand clinical credibility, ingredient transparency, and substance over hype. The skills you build here translate directly into careers in medical device sales, pharmaceutical sales, biotech, healthcare technology, luxury goods, or any field where you need to sell credibly to educated, high-value buyers.If you’ve ever wondered what real B2B sales looks like at a luxury brand — the prospecting, the pitch development, the in-person demos, the follow-ups, the close — this is where you’ll learn it. Not from a textbook. From doing it. Why This Role Is ExcitingYou’ll get to immerse yourself in everything beauty, skincare, and clinical formulation. You’ll talk about active ingredients, formulation chemistry, brand positioning, and the science behind why our products work. You’ll meet practitioners doing some of the most interesting work in aesthetic medicine. You’ll walk into med spas, dermatology clinics, and plastic surgery practices and represent a brand you genuinely believe in.If you love skincare and beauty, this is a dream role. You’re not selling something you have to justify — you’re selling a clinical luxury product you’d want yourself.And you’ll do it alongside a founder who has done this before, who will teach you the playbook in real time, and who will invest in your growth because your success is the brand’s success. What You’ll DoSales Pipeline Development• Research and identify target accounts: med spas, dermatology practices, plastic surgery offices, and aesthetic clinics in the Portland metro and beyond• Build and maintain the CRM pipeline in HubSpot• Schedule in-person demos and follow-up appointments• Prepare account-specific outreach materials and pitch decksIn-Person Demos and Founder Shadowing• Accompany the Founder & CEO on in-person sales demos to clinical accounts• Observe and gradually participate in founder-led sales conversations• Take detailed notes on objections, questions, and conversion patterns• Build the muscle memory of clinical B2B sales by watching it executed liveCustomer and Partner Communication TrainingYou will be trained to speak credibly with the most sophisticated buyers in beauty — board-certified dermatologists, plastic surgeons, NPs, and aesthetic medical spa owners. This is a skill that takes most early-career salespeople years to develop. You’ll get hands-on training from someone who has sold to executives, physicians, and sovereign clients across multiple industries.Pilot Program and Account Management• Support the paid clinical pilot program — onboarding new pilot accounts, tracking 30-day milestones, scheduling follow-up calls• Maintain account communications and ensure no opportunity falls through the cracks• Help convert pilot accounts into full wholesale relationships• Document case studies, testimonials, and clinical feedback from converted accountsStrategic Sales Projects• Contribute to corporate gifting outreach for the holiday season• Support sales materials development including the clinical leave-behind, pitch deck refinements, and email templates• Research and identify multi-location group opportunities (med spa groups with 5+ locations)• Help shape the sales playbook in real time as the brand scales What We’re Looking For• Entrepreneurial spirit — you take initiative, you’re comfortable in unstructured environments, and you move fast• Genuine skincare and beauty enthusiast — you actually care about the industry, the science, and the brands shaping it• Strong interpersonal skills — comfortable in professional settings, confident in conversation, able to read a room• Excellent verbal and written communication — clear, articulate, and credible when speaking with highly educated buyers• Coachability — you want to learn, you welcome feedback, and you apply it quickly• Resilience and self-direction — you don’t need to be micromanaged, and you handle a “no” without losing momentum• Working knowledge of AI tools — Claude, ChatGPT, or similar; you use them to research accounts, draft outreach, and work efficiently• Currently enrolled in college with the ability to receive academic credit for the internship• Reliable transportation preferred for in-person Portland metro field work, though not strictly required Who This Is Perfect ForThis internship is an exceptional fit if you’re studying or interested in:• Sales, marketing, or business development• Medical device or pharmaceutical sales (this role builds the foundation)• Healthcare, biotech, or life sciences• Skincare, beauty, or aesthetic medicine• Pre-med, pre-PA, or pre-nursing (with interest in commercial healthcare)• Entrepreneurship, brand-building, or luxury goodsYou do not need prior sales experience. You need curiosity, drive, professionalism, and a real interest in the industry. The skills will be taught. What You’ll Walk Away With• Hands-on training in real B2B clinical sales from an experienced CRO/CEO• The ability to speak credibly with physician-owners, dermatologists, and aesthetic medical professionals — a skill set that translates directly into medical device and pharmaceutical sales careers• A portfolio of closed accounts, pipeline development, and sales materials you contributed to• A direct mentorship relationship with a seasoned exited entrepreneur• Real-world sales process experience: prospecting, pitching, demoing, objection-handling, closing, account management• Professional references from a real launch• A first-look offer for a paid part-time or full-time role at Volaant after September 20 How to ApplySend the following to hello@volaant.com with the subject line “Sales Consultant Application – [Your Name]”:1. A brief introduction (3–5 sentences) on why this role is right for you and why you’re passionate about beauty2. Resume or LinkedIn profile3. One example of a time you communicated, persuaded, or led someone to a decision - could be from school, a campus role, a previous job, a side project, fundraising, or anything that demonstrates your ability to influence and follow through4. 3-5 minute video on why you want to work with us, and your favorite skincare or beauty brand and why5. Your city and confirmation that you have reliable transportationApplications reviewed on a rolling basis. Start date is immediate - we are hiring through mid June and onboarding new consultants weekly. 30 total roles which will remain open until filled.

jobs.title: 🎬 PAID REMOTE INTERNSHIP: TikTok & Instagram POV Content Creator at cyberklick
jobs.description: Employer: cyberklick Expires: 07/12/2026 🎬 PAID REMOTE INTERNSHIP: TikTok & Instagram POV Content CreatorPosition: Social Media Content Creator (Intern)Location: 100% Remote (Create from anywhere!)Schedule: Completely Flexible (Work on your own time)Are you obsessed with TikTok trends and IG Reels? Do you know how to hook an audience in the first 3 seconds using the "POV" (Point of View) format? Stop scrolling for free and start getting paid for your creativity! jobs.description: Employer: Cincinnati Symphony Orchestra Expires: 07/13/2026 Artistic Planning Intern Position Summary: Reporting directly to the Manager of Artistic Planning & Assistant to the Music Director, this internship provides a strong knowledge base in guest artist logistics and servicing, season planning, administering contracts, completing artist research and working cross departmentally to execute a production. This is an ideal experience for someone pursuing a career in artistic planning and/or artist management. Duties and Responsibilities: Assisting with guest artist logistics and backstage hospitality for rehearsals and performances.Working with Artistic Department staff to create and distribute guest artist itineraries.Complete research projects on topics useful to Artistic staff for planning purposes. Learn about and contribute ideas related to season planning.Learn about and draft guest artist contracts and commissioning agreements. Participate in departmental and interdepartmental meetings and coordinate with other departments on Artistic department-related needs.Contribute to the CSO's vision to be the most relevant orchestra in America. Represent the organization's values in your work. Attend professional development sessions.Other related duties required as part of a team environment. Candidate Requirements:College Junior, Senior, Graduate Student, or those who recently graduated with an expressed interest in Artistic PlanningStrong written and verbal communications skillsKnowledge of classical and/or choral music Project management experience The CSO encourages those that have been historically underrepresented in orchestral music to apply. Compensation: $15.25 per hour

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Social Media Intern at HarborBlue Vacation Rentals
jobs.description: Employer: HarborBlue Vacation Rentals Expires: 07/13/2026 About HarborBlueHarborBlue Vacation Rentals is a boutique vacation rental management company serving Cape Cod. We're currently a small, fast-growing, high-touch operation that takes pride in delivering a seamless, stress-free experience for both our homeowners and guests. We're looking to promote our brand to homeowners (whose properties we would manage) and guests (who we are looking to attract to our homeowners' properties). To achieve this, we want to build out our social media presence (Instagram, Facebook) from ground zero. About the Social Media & Marketing Intern RoleWe're looking for a creative, self-directed Social Media & Marketing Intern to own HarborBlue's social presence for the summer season. You'll produce roughly 30 posts per month across Instagram and Facebook. These posts will be a mix of photos, carousels, and Reels. You'll have a voice in ideating and selecting content: anything from beautiful Cape Cod properties, interviews of local businesses, and / or behind-the-scenes clips of a growing hospitality company. You'll work directly with the founder, shoot on-location content at properties across the Cape, and see your work drive real business results. What You'll DoPlan, create, and schedule ~30 posts per month across Instagram and Facebook (photos, carousels, and short-form video/Reels)Shoot on-property photo and video content at vacation homes around the CapeWrite engaging captions in HarborBlue's brand voice and maintain a monthly content calendarEdit short-form video using CapCut, Instagram tools, or similarCreate branded graphics in Canva consistent with our visual identityEngage with our communityResearch hashtags, trends, and local events to keep content timely and discoverableTrack performance metrics (reach, engagement, follower growth) and report monthly on what's workingSupport broader marketing projects as needed — email content, blog posts, listing photographyWho You AreCurrently enrolled in (or recently graduated from) a college program, ideally in marketing, communications, hospitality, or a related fieldActive and fluent on Instagram - you understand Reels, trends, and what makes content performComfortable shooting and editing photo/video on a smartphone; an eye for composition and lightStrong writer with attention to detail and the ability to match a brand voiceSelf-starter who can manage a content calendar with minimal supervisionReliable transportation and a valid driver's license (you'll travel to properties across the Cape; mileage reimbursed)Based on or near Cape Cod for the summerBonus PointsExperience managing a brand, business, or club social accountPhotography or videography portfolioFamiliarity with scheduling tools (Later, Buffer, Meta Business Suite)Interest in real estate, hospitality, or entrepreneurshipCompensation & DetailsPay: $18–$22/hour, depending on experience (W-2 seasonal employee)Hours: 12–15 hours per week, flexible schedulingDuration: June through August/September, with potential to extend into the school year part-timeLocation: Hybrid — remote content planning plus on-location shoots across the Upper and Mid-CapePerks: Mileage reimbursement for property visits; direct mentorship from the founder; a portfolio of real published work and measurable results

Project management experience. The CSO encourages those that have been historically underrepresented in orchestral music to apply. Compensation: $15.25 per hour

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Content Creator Intern at LNG Landscapes
jobs.description: Employer: LNG Landscapes Expires: 07/13/2026 Every company posting one of these wants a “social media guru” or a “content ninja.” We don't. We want someone who shows up, films, and ships. So we're going to be straight about what this is and what it isn't. LNG Landscapes is one of the fastest-growing landscape design and build companies in the Twin Cities. We turn dirt patches into backyards families use for the next 20 years. The work is genuinely good — your job is to make sure the internet sees it. WHAT WE'RE OFFERING: → Real creative ownership. You pitch the idea, you shoot it, you cut it, it goes live. You're not filling in someone else's template — if your content is good, the whole metro sees it. → Content people actually stop for. You're not posting stock photos of grass. You're on real job sites capturing real transformations. The footage is good because the work is good — you just have to capture it. → The best tools, including AI. A modern editing stack and AI tools we'll train you on so you punch way above your experience level. We don't ask anyone here to work with second-rate gear. → Direct mentorship from the founder. You'll see how content, paid ads, email, SMS, and Google Business all fit together at a company that's actually scaling — not from a textbook, from the inside. → A real portfolio. Published, professional work you can point to in any future interview. Not class projects. → $18/hr, 10–12 hours/week, flexible around your class schedule — one set in-person field day, the rest on your own time. WHAT WE'RE NOT OFFERING: → A remote gig you phone in from your laptop. This is in-person and in the field — the whole point is that you film the ideas you come up with. That doesn't happen from a couch. → A passive seat. If you want a checklist handed to you and to be left alone, that's fine, just not here. We move fast and want someone who keeps up. WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO:Film and photograph projects, crews, and before/afters. Edit short-form video and photo for Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and the website. Run our social accounts and Google Business Profile. Help build creative for paid ads, email, and SMS campaigns. Pitch your own ideas, then go shoot them. WHO THIS IS FOR:A student or early-career creative with a real eye — someone already making content they're proud of, even if it's just their own page. Comfortable on a camera and in editing tools (CapCut, Premiere, Canva, whatever you use). Local to the east Twin Cities metro with reliable transportation to job sites. Curious about marketing, brand, and AI. Interest in landscaping or the trades is a plus, not a requirement. TO APPLY:Apply with your best work — a reel, a TikTok, an edit, a portfolio link, anything. We care far more about what you can make than your résumé. If a class project is the best thing you've got, send it; if it's a video you made for fun, even better.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Interior Design Intern at Harley Ellis Devereaux (HED)
jobs.description: Employer: Harley Ellis Devereaux (HED) Expires: 07/13/2026 About HED: We are a team that is full of ideas, experience, creativity, passionate opinions, insatiable curiosity, uncompromising integrity, commitment, and skill. Our culture is about aspiration, embracing change and challenges, listening and learning from each other, encouraging continual learning, and inspiring collective growth. As an inclusive, integrated architecture and engineering practice, we value the diversity of perspectives, experiences, abilities, and expertise that advance both the work we do, and the world we share.HED | IDEAS – Interior Design InternHED | IDEAS (Intern Development for Engineering and Architecture Students) is an approximate 10-week program that provides aspiring A/E professionals real-world work experiences that introduces them to the many diverse components of an A/E firm. The experience is intended to complement and support their academic learning experience. Interns contribute to one or more of HED’s nine market sectors. Project work exposure can vary, from planning and concept development, through design and production and construction administration. Interns also attend a series of workshops that provide introductions to the intricacies of integrated practice, project management, programming, business development and marketing, all crucial to the successful operations of an A/E firm. Interns at HED harness their critical thinking skills, enthusiasm, innovation, passion, energy, and curiosity to make a positive impact through design excellence and contributing to making the world a better place. They thrive in a collaborative and hybrid work environment that embraces authenticity, discipline, growth, change and making a mark on the world. Position Summary:As an Interior Design Intern at HED, you are beginning your professional experience by contributing to interior design projects through research, documentation, and creative support. Working under direct supervision, you assist project teams with day-to-day design tasks while gaining exposure to an integrated design environment. This role is designed to provide hands-on learning that complements academic training, preparing you for a future role in professional interior design practice. Essential Functions: Assist with material research, product sourcing, and FF&E documentation.Support design team with drafting, redlines, and updates to presentation materials.Participate in internal team meetings and take notes for reference and coordination.Create visual assets such as diagrams, material boards, or renderings under guidance.Help maintain sample library and organize digital reference files.Observe and learn Revit workflows and coordination with architectural teams.Seek feedback and ask questions to support professional development.Requirements Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s program in Interior Design or Interior Architecture.Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and familiarity with Revit or other design software.Strong verbal and written communication skills.Enthusiastic attitude, curiosity, and collaborative mindset.Portfolio or coursework samples that demonstrate creativity and foundational skills. Attaching a portfolio is required for consideration.Physical Requirements:Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.Must be able to effectively communicate in writing and verbally via phone, video conferencing, and in person.Must be able to assist with field verification and site visits.Visual acuity to perform responsibilities.Work Environment:We embrace a hybrid model that promotes both autonomy and collaboration, including the freedom to work from home, with regular in-office days to connect with teammates and build culture.To be considered, a pdf file of your portfolio must be submitted with your application.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Community Intern, Pittsburgh at Yelp
jobs.description: Employer: Yelp Expires: 07/13/2026 SummaryYelp’s Community team works in markets across the United States and Canada to bring attention to the best local businesses in their area. Through events, local partnerships, and social media, our team builds relationships with small business owners and uses their behind-the-scenes experience to show other customers what makes small businesses so special. Join the fun and begin building your five star career as a Community Intern with Yelp! You will engage in a 6-month development program, equipped with the support of both a cohort of peers spanning the US and Canada and the 1:1 mentorship from your local Community Manager. You will play an integral part in supporting activities designed to rally the Yelp community both online and off. You will gain tactical experience in social media management, event planning, and online community building, while building excitement around what's happening in your own community. What you'll do:You will work with your Community Manager to understand your market and prioritize effective messagingYou will help to plan and execute events including remarkable parties, local meet-ups, and happy hoursYou will gain practical experience in digital and social media marketing through creating and organizing social media content that aligns with regional goals around growing channels and bolstering engagement You will support the maintenance of Yelp’s local online business listings via our app and website by flagging photos, identifying media alerts and scams, and ensuring accurate business informationYou will support online community building through engaging with Yelpers via the app/website in a variety of ways What it takes to succeed:You are a current undergraduate student, college graduate, or interested in making a career change to a community building role.You currently reside in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania and have reliable transportation (Required)You are at least 21 years of age (Required) You consider yourself a local expert- you know what is trending in the area and have a love for small businessesYou have experience and interest in planning and coordinating eventsYou have strong written and verbal communication skillsYou are well organized and pay attention to detailYou have experience with social media copywriting and asset coordination You are a creative problem solver who understands Yelp’s applications You have the bandwidth and flexibility to work 10-19 hours per week, Monday through Friday with the potential for some weekend workYou have the ability to lift 10 pounds without assistance What you'll get:Compensation range for this position is $13.00 - $16.00 per hour.

You will play an integral part in supporting activities designed to rally the Yelp community both online and off. You will gain tactical experience in social media management, event planning, and online community building, while building excitement around what's happening in your own community. What you'll do:You will work with your Community Manager to understand your market and prioritize effective messagingYou will help to plan and execute events including remarkable parties, local meet-ups, and happy hoursYou will gain practical experience in digital and social media marketing through creating and organizing social media content that aligns with regional goals around growing channels and bolstering engagement You will support the maintenance of Yelp’s local online business listings via our app and website by flagging photos, identifying media alerts and scams, and ensuring accurate business informationYou will support online community building through engaging with Yelpers via the app/website in a variety of ways What it takes to succeed:You are a current undergraduate student, college graduate, or interested in making a career change to a community building role.You currently reside in Cincinnati, Ohio, and have reliable transportation (Required)You are at least 21 years of age (Required) You consider yourself a local expert- you know what is trending in the area and have a love for small businessesYou have experience and interest in planning and coordinating eventsYou have strong written and verbal communication skillsYou are well organized and pay attention to detailYou have experience with social media copywriting and asset coordination You are a creative problem solver who understands Yelp’s applications You have the bandwidth and flexibility to work 10-19 hours per week, Monday through Friday with the potential for some weekend workYou have the ability to lift 10 pounds without assistance What you'll get:Compensation range for this position is $13.00 - $16.00 per hour.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Science Communication Intern at The Tech
jobs.description: Employer: The Tech Expires: 07/13/2026 Science Communication & Social Media Intern (Fall)Who We Are Since opening in 1998, The Tech Interactive has been an integral part of the San Francisco Bay area and is proud to serve families and young people through hands-on activities, experimental labs, and design challenge experiences. Here at The Tech our mission is to inspire the innovator in everyone! We are a team of diverse individuals committed to creating learning experiences that empower young folks to innovate with creativity, curiosity, and compassion. We believe that technology is leading and transforming lives all over the world and we want to help develop the next generation of problem-solvers. If you join our team, we know you will have a fulfilling and rewarding role focused on giving back to our community, especially those that have been historically marginalized and underserved. Program Overview The Tech Interactive is seeking a creative, motivated, and digitally savvy intern to help transform hands-on STEM experiences into engaging social media content. This internship provides college students with an opportunity to apply storytelling, digital media, marketing, and communication skills in a museum and non-profit educational environment. As a Social Media Content Creation Intern, you will develop short-form video content that highlights science, technology, engineering, and design experiences for diverse audiences across social media platforms. Working alongside marketing, education, and content professionals, you will gain hands-on experience in content strategy, video production, audience engagement, and science communication. Learning Outcomes Through this internship, students will:Develop professional skills in digital content creation and social media marketingApply storytelling techniques to communicate STEM concepts to public audiencesGain experience in video production, editing, and content strategyLearn how audience insights and platform analytics inform content decisionsBuild a professional portfolio of published content and creative projectsApply classroom knowledge to real-world projects in a professional STEM nonprofit environment Key Responsibilities: Content Creation & ProductionProduce content that aligns with organizational brand standards and audience engagement goalsCreate and edit short-form videos for TikTok (approximately 3–5 pieces per week)Capture footage of exhibits, engineering challenges, design activities, educational programs, and museum experiencesAssist with on-camera presentations, interviews, demonstrations, and behind-the-scenes storytellingSTEM Storytelling & Science CommunicationTranslate STEM concepts into accessible, engaging, and audience-friendly contentHighlight real-world applications of science, technology, engineering, and designCollaborate with educators and subject matter experts to ensure accuracy and educational valueConduct research to verify STEM-related content and maintain factual integritySocial Media Strategy & Audience EngagementMonitor social media trends, platform best practices, and emerging content formats to inform content strategyDevelop engaging captions, creative concepts, and recurring content series that increase audience reach and engagementSupport community management by interacting with audiences and fostering meaningful online engagementAnalyze content performance metrics, share insights and recommendations, and participate in team meetings and professional development activities Required QualificationsMust be a current undergraduate in Communications, Marketing, Media Studies, Journalism, Digital Media, Film, Education, STEM-related fields, or a related disciplineStrong interest in social media, digital storytelling, and content creationExcellent communication and collaboration skillsAbility to manage multiple projects and meet deadlinesCuriosity about science, technology, engineering, design, or informal learning Preferred QualificationsExperience creating content for TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, or similar platformsFamiliarity with video editing software such as CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or equivalent toolsExperience with photography, videography, or visual storytellingInterest in making STEM education accessible, engaging, and inclusive Working Conditions and ScheduleInternship will be 10 weeks in the Fall for 15 - 20 hours per week, with flexible scheduling based on operational need/availability; the internship will take place from September 16 - December 4, 2026. This is an on-site role with the possibility of flexibility for working some days at an alternative work location. Flexibility will vary depending on the needs of the job.Working occasional evenings and weekends as needed.Frequently moves objects up to 50 pounds. Application ProcessPlease submit 2 to 5 examples of relevant content or creative work (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, portfolio samples, etc.)Candidates will be reviewed on a rolling basis in the order applications are received. Priority consideration will be given to applications submitted by July 1, 2026. Applications received after this date may not receive full consideration, though the position will remain open until filled. Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, and Accessibility (IDEA) at The TechOur success is dependent on building teams that include people from different backgrounds and experiences who challenge each other’s assumptions with fresh perspectives. To that end, we look for a diverse pool of applicants including but not limited to those from historically marginalized groups — women, people with disabilities, people of color, people who are lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and/or nonconforming, veterans, and people from different socioeconomic backgrounds. The Tech will provide an equal opportunity in recruitment and employment to all individuals and will not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), gender identity, gender expression, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, legally protected medical condition, military or veteran status, marital status, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law. Any applicant with a disability may contact The Tech Interactive to arrange for accommodations. Please contact the Tech at careers@thetech.org or call (408) 795-6248. The pay range for this role is:19 - 19 USD per hour

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Entry-Level Marketing Internship-Leadership Track at Resilient Enterprises Inc
jobs.description: Employer: Resilient Enterprises Inc Expires: 07/14/2026 Job Title: Entry-Level Marketing Internship – Leadership TrackLocation: Boise, IDCompensation: Paid Internship (Plus performance based incentives) About the OpportunityLooking to kickstart your career in marketing, business, or management?Our Entry-Level Marketing Internship is designed for individuals who want more than just experience — it’s built for those looking to develop leadership skills and grow into future management roles. This hands-on program provides real-world exposure to marketing campaigns, client relations, and team leadership. What You’ll DoAssist in executing marketing campaigns for nonprofit clients Engage with customers through face-to-face outreach and promotions Develop communication and presentation skills in real-time environments Track campaign performance and contribute to daily team goals Work closely with leadership to learn business operations and strategy Leadership Track OverviewInterns who demonstrate strong performance and consistency will have the opportunity to:Take on team leadership responsibilities Assist in training and mentoring new team members Learn the fundamentals of managing campaigns and people Be considered for full-time roles in leadership and management What You’ll GainHands-on marketing and business experience Leadership development and management training Resume-building experience with measurable results Professional networking opportunities A clear path toward full-time career advancement Who We’re Looking ForMotivated, goal-oriented individuals Strong communication and people skills Interest in marketing, business, or leadership development Ability to work in a fast-paced, team environment No prior experience required — full training provided Why This Internship Stands OutThis is not a typical internship. You won’t be observing — you’ll be actively involved in campaigns, developing real skills, and building a foundation for long-term career growth. Apply today to gain real experience and start building your path toward leadership.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Seasonal Education and Events Intern- Cradle of Forestry Session C at FIND Outdoors
jobs.description: Employer: FIND Outdoors Expires: 07/14/2026 About the Opportunity: Seasonal Education and Events Intern Cradle of Forestry Pisgah Forest, NC August 2026- October 2026 RESPONSIBILITIES: Assist in planning and executing summer and fall site events.Collaborate with FIND staff, volunteers, and partners (including the U.S. Forest Service) to ensure successful events.Lead and facilitate educational programs and interpretive activities for participants of all ages and group sizes, both indoors and outdoors. Prepare and guide groups on trails, both paved and natural, using the surrounding forest as a classroom to achieve educational goals. Maintain and clean interactive exhibits, conduct data collection, and ensure displays are up to date in all program areas. Utilize and distribute Natural Inquirer educational materials. Provide general customer service and information to visitors, serving as a positive representative of FIND Outdoors. Collaborate with the Cradle Educator, Site Manager and volunteers to ensure effective communication and program execution. Perform other related duties as assigned. SKILLS & ABILITIES: Comfortable working with diverse groups of all ages and delivering engaging educational experiences. Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a visitor-centered attitude.Physical ability to walk up to four miles daily and lift up to 20 pounds.Knowledge of ecosystems, forest ecology, and local wildlife is a plus. Professional and friendly interaction with the public, ensuring a positive image for FIND Outdoors. Flexibility to work nights, weekends, and varying hours, especially during special events, which may require frequent evening or weekend shifts. QUALIFICATIONS: A combination of academic studies and practical experience in environmental education, event planning, or related fields. Experience in public speaking or leading groups.Ability to collaborate effectively with other team members and work independently when required. A passion for environmental education, event planning, and outdoor recreation. LEARNING OUTCOMES: Gain valuable experience in designing, implementing, and facilitating educational programming and events. Develop skills in public speaking, group management, and community outreach. Deepen your knowledge of forest ecology, conservation, flora and fauna. Experience working in a dynamic, team-oriented environment with the opportunity for hands-on learning and professional development. INTERNSHIP STRUCTURE*: Duration: August to October, 2026 (12 weeks)Work Schedule: 20-24 hours/weekly, with some evening shifts required during special events. Lodging: On-site shared dorm lodging is available at the Cradle of Forestry for the duration of the internship if needed. Stipend: A stipend will be provided to support living expenses during the internship. *May adjust for college intern credit requirements

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Digital Marketing Strategy Internship at WebFX
jobs.description: Employer: WebFX Expires: 07/15/2026 Hi there! We're WebFX, a full-service digital marketing agency based in the US. We've been 10x named the Best Place To Work in Pennsylvania, and we'd love to meet you! We are a fast-growing company that has doubled in size over the past 5 years, with talented team members now based around the globe (and representing 18+ different countries!). While we are growing at a rapid rate, we are committed to growing strategically and sustainably, and that starts with growing our team of the #BestCoworkers - that’s where you come in!We're looking for people to join our mission to provide world-class digital marketing solutions to mid-size businesses around the world. jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Democracy Focused Creative Content Creator Intern at GAIN POWER
jobs.description: Employer: GAIN POWER Expires: 07/15/2026 Must Apply Here: https://gainpower.link/HSdesignintern JOB TITLE: Creative Intern(s) — Graphic Design, Video & Content Creation ORGANIZATION: GAIN Power LOCATION: Remote JOB TYPE: Part-time / Internship SALARY: $20/hourABOUT GAIN POWERGAIN Power is a 22-year-old professional community and marketplace for people working in Democratic politics, elections, and civic advocacy. We connect campaign staff, political consultants, field organizers, data professionals, and advocacy leaders with the jobs, resources, and relationships they need to win.Our community includes 6,000+ vetted members and reaches 39,000+ democracy professionals through our newsletters and job board. We run the Democracy Directory, a job board with salary transparency, virtual career fairs, RootsCamp, the Powerful IDEA Awards, and a growing suite of training and professional development resources for the field.We're based in Cambridge, MA, and currently rebuilding our platform and brand from the ground up. THE ROLEWe're looking for one or more creative interns to help us tell GAIN Power's story. You'll work directly with our founder, Amy Pritchard, to produce graphics, video, and social content that reaches the people on the front lines of democracy — campaign managers, field directors, organizers, and advocates running races across the country in 2026.This isn't busy work. The content you make will be seen by tens of thousands of democracy professionals. You'll also support the creative needs of our programs and training offerings — helping us produce materials that educate, inspire, and move people to action.If you care about politics, love making things look good, and want your work to matter — this is the role for you.WHAT YOU'LL DODesign graphics, flyers, and digital assets for social media, email, and the webEdit short-form video for Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, and YouTubeCreate visual and video content for GAIN Power's training programs and professional development offeringsSupport content creation for our platform launch, career fairs, and eventsHelp shape and maintain a consistent visual identity across all channelsAppear on camera for reels or talking-head content (if comfortable — not required)Bring your own creative ideas and execute on them quicklyWrite brief weekly content summaries for the founderWHAT WE'RE LOOKING FORProficiency across multiple creative tools — we need someone comfortable in both design and video, whether that's Canva + CapCut, Adobe Creative Suite + Descript, or a similar combinationA portfolio or work samples that show your range across both design and videoStrong creative instincts and attention to detailAbility to work independently in a remote, fast-moving environmentInterest in civic engagement, democracy, or progressive causesBONUS POINTS FORHands-on experience with CapCut or Descript specificallyMotion graphics or animation skillsContent created for political campaigns, advocacy orgs, or nonprofitsExperience producing educational or training contentComfort on camera for reels or talking-head videoA track record growing or managing a social media accountDETAILSPay: $20/hourHours: 10–15 hours per week, flexible around your scheduleDuration: Spring/Summer 2026, with potential to continueStart date: As soon as possibleWork style: Fully remote with occasional optional in-person meetups in Cambridge, MABEYOND THIS ROLEWe want to invest in you — not just your output. Strong candidates we're unable to hire may be connected with other organizations in our network hiring for creative and communications roles. We run a virtual career fair in June that connects democracy professionals with campaigns, advocacy orgs, and nonprofits hiring now, and we offer training opportunities for people looking to grow in the democracy space. Let us know in your application if you're interested — we're happy to help.TO APPLYComplete our application form here. Skip the formal cover letter — we want to hear from you directly. We respond to everyone.GAIN Power is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We strongly encourage applications from people of color, women, LGBTQ+ individuals, and candidates from communities historically underrepresented in politics and tech.

jobs.description: Employer: WebFX Expires: 07/15/2026 Hi there! We're WebFX, a full-service digital marketing agency based in the US. We've been 10x named the Best Place To Work in Pennsylvania, and we'd love to meet you! We are a fast-growing company that has doubled in size over the past 5 years, with talented team members now based around the globe (and representing 18+ different countries!). While we are growing at a rapid rate, we are committed to growing strategically and sustainably, and that starts with growing our team of the #BestCoworkers - that’s where you come in!We're looking for people to join our mission to provide world-class digital marketing solutions to mid-size businesses around the world. We are passionate about what we do, we’re committed to driving business growth for our clients, and we’re on the hunt for people just like you, who take pride in their work and want to be part of a company that does too.You Might Be a Great Fit For This Internship if You Have…A Bachelor’s Degree (either in-progress or completed)Past interns have majored in Marketing, Advertising, Business, Journalism, Communications, Information Systems, Statistics, and beyond GPA above 3.4A Few Related Skills and ExperiencesCustomer service experience in any industryOutstanding written and verbal communication skillsDigital marketing experienceVery basic HTML experienceExcel/Google Docs skillsAnalytical/research skillsEagerness to learn and be trained!Facebook account with over 100 friends (Only used to verify web understanding)Twitter account with over 25 followers (Only used to verify web understanding)Stays up to date on new changes to the web and actively reads several blogs Any of these Signature FXer Traits!You have an interest in the web and stay up-to-date on new and developing technologiesYou have an eye for detail and dedication to high-quality workYou are a professional, dependable, and independent worker with a strong work ethicYou’re self-motivated, thrive on challenges, and enjoy getting things doneYou are a proactive, creative problem-solver who faces challenges with a can-do mindsetYou possess excellent time management skillsYou work with a sense of urgency and can consistently meet deadlinesYou are a lifelong learner who loves to grow and stretch outside of your comfort zone, and are always looking to improve your skillsIf any of these sound like you, then we want to hear from you! We are committed to growing 1% better everyday, and we believe working at WebFX could quite possibly make your life 1(00)% better - after all, you would be surrounded by the #BestCoworkersinPA!In This Internship, You’ll Get To…-Consult with social media specialists on a regular basis to discuss client feedback, create content, and report on social media campaign performance-Research and create online “buyer personas”-Create programs that leverage earned, owned, and paid media elements-Develop social media editorial calendars-Assist in the development of creative/assets for social channels-Ability to manage content creation workflow independentlyA Few Extra Details!-This is an in-person internship, based at our riverside campus in Harrisburg, PA!-Must be able to work at least 10 hours per week-This internship is flexible with hours and days - we will work with our interns to determine a schedule that is a mutual fit-Interns will enjoy their own workspace, and computer, along with the ability to wear jeans/casual dress to work each day-Letters of recommendation are provided along with the ability to learn valuable digital marketing skills-We are always open to considering interns for potential full-time roles after graduation as well!What You’ll Get From Us!Opportunities to Learn and Train With Our Team!-Interns will receive world-class on-the-job training from the experts (think Sr. Marketers, not the HR team) as well as opportunities for ongoing personal learning and development-On-site, state-of-the-art training amenities to facilitate departmental trainings, industry-related updates, and monthly Lunch-and-Learns.Compensation$17-$18/hourWhy Choose WebFX?- We've been named the Best Place To Work in Pennsylvania 10 times 🎉- We're expanding! Cool, so do we! That's why we have a Pet Friendly Office 🐶- Profit Sharing 💰 - Need that caffeine fix? On-site cappuccino machine, Little Amps Coffee Bar (with a barista!) & Tea Bar ☕- Looking for a little extra workday fuel? Enjoy surprise catered breakfasts, lunches, mid-day snacks, and more!- On-site Yoga sessions- On-site Fitness Center 🏋️‍♀️- 150% Company Match Of Personal Charity Donations- Our #FXBuilds program is set to positively impact 10,000 people around the world by 2024 - and every individual FXer’s work directly contributes! 🌍- Supplemental Insurance- 100% Company Match 401K (up to 4%) 💰 - Generous Paid Time Off 🏖- Employee Wellness Program, including a free FitBit and fitness challenges 👟- Love to learn?
